title: KORG phase8 – Acoustic Synthesizer
author: bpierre
summary:
Acoustic Synthesis uses physically vibrating bodies to generate sound, enhanced with the electronic control you’d expect from a synthesizer. The result is an instrument that produces sound which feels alive and responds to physical interactions such as touch and acoustic feedback.


It is beyond “analogue vs digital”. It’s even beyond electronics.

8 independent electromechanical voices with steel resonators are at the heart of the instrument’s acoustic sound.
phase8 comes with 13 chromatically tuned resonators; 8 of your choice can be installed at any one time. Through envelope control, these resonators can produce everything from short percussive sounds to long, drawn-out sustained notes.

The design allows you to easily swap and tune resonators, letting you customise the scale and character of your phase8.

An intuitive sequencer supports both step programming and unquantized live recording. Each voice offers step skip for polymetric sequencing.

All sequences can be saved and recalled across 8 memory slots.
You can cycle between three distinct amplitude modulation effects: tremolo and two audio rate, pitch-dependent modulation types. The last modulation effect can be optionally harmonically quantised.
Using the instrument’s shift knob, you can add delayed triggers to the resonators relative to the selected or synced tempo.
All controls on the instrument’s panel can be automated over a sequence using the record function.
Beyond adjusting parameters, phase8 invites physical interaction. Sculpt sound by touching, plucking, strumming, or tapping the resonators – or experiment by adding found objects for new textures.

The AIR slider lets you boost (or quiet) the acoustic response of whatever you bring in contact with the instrument.

Both MIDI / USB-MIDI and CV can be used to externally control the knob parameters.

phase8 can be tempo synchronized to other devices over MIDI / USB-MIDI and Sync.

External MIDI devices can be used to trigger notes in the Acoustic Synth, and the sequencer can trigger notes in external MIDI instruments over MIDI / USB-MIDI. These connectivity options allow many possibilities for phase8 to integrate into any creative setup.
The phase8 presale exclusive package includes three selected limited-edition percussive resonators. Created through experimentation beyond the standard chromatic set, these resonators are designed for open-ended, tactile sound exploration—each offering its own distinctive percussive character.

Available exclusively as part of the presale package.
content:
# 🎹 KORG phase8 – The Acoustic Synthesizer That Feels Alive  

*By bpierre – 27 points on Hacker News*  

[Source](https://www.korg.com/us/products/dj/phase8/)

---

## What Is the phase8?

The **KORG phase8** isn’t just another synthesizer.  
It blends **physical vibration** with classic electronic control, giving you a keyboard that *responds* to the way you touch it. In other words, you get sound that feels organic, yet you still have all the precision and flexibility you’d expect from a modern synth.

---

## Key Features – Plain and Simple  

| Feature | Why It Matters |
| ------- | --------------- |
| **8 independent electromechanical voices** | Each voice has its own steel resonator, so the instrument can produce eight distinct sounds at once. |
| **13 chromatically‑tuned resonators** (pick any 8) | Swap resonators easily to change scale, timbre, or character on the fly. |
| **Envelope control** | Shape each note from a tight, percussive click to a long, evolving tone. |
| **Intuitive sequencer** | Step‑program or record live, with step‑skip for polymetric grooves. |
| **8 memory slots** | Store entire sequences and recall them instantly. |
| **Three amplitude‑modulation effects** | Tremolo + two audio‑rate, pitch‑dependent modulations (one can be harmonically quantised). |
| **Shift‑knob‑driven delay** | Add rhythmic offsets to resonators that stay in sync with the tempo. |
| **Full automation** | Record any panel control into a sequence for evolving performances. |
| **Physical interaction** | Touch, pluck, strum, tap, or even attach found objects to sculpt sound. |
| **AIR slider** | Boost or mute the acoustic response of whatever is contacting the resonators. |
| **MIDI, USB‑MIDI & CV** | Control parameters and trigger notes from external gear; sync tempo with other devices. |
| **Presale exclusive package** | Comes with three limited‑edition percussive resonators for experimental play. |

---

## How It Works: Acoustic Meets Electronic  

1. **Vibrating Steel Bars** – Each resonator is a piece of steel that physically vibrates when struck.  
2. **Electronic Envelope & Modulation** – Sensors read the vibration and let the synth shape the sound (attack, decay, tremolo, etc.).  
3. **Hands‑On Performance** – Because the sound starts in the metal, you can **pluck**, **tap**, or **scratch** it just like a real instrument. Add a piece of wood, a metal spoon, or anything you find—new timbres appear instantly.  

The result is a hybrid instrument that feels more like a drum or xylophone than a typical keyboard, yet you still have full control over pitch, rhythm, and modulation.

---

## Why Musicians Might Love It  

- **Expressive Playability** – The tactile feedback encourages a more “performer‑centric” approach.  
- **Customizable Tonality** – Swap resonators to match any scale or mood without rewiring.  
- **Hybrid Workflow** – Use the built‑in sequencer for patterns, then hand‑play over them for organic variation.  
- **Studio & Stage Friendly** – MIDI / USB‑MIDI and CV connectivity let the phase8 sit in any DAW, modular rig, or live rig.  

---

## Quick Getting‑Started Checklist  

- ✅ Choose 8 resonators that fit your musical key.  
- ✅ Set envelope parameters for the desired attack/decay.  
- ✅ Program a basic step‑sequence or record a live groove.  
- ✅ Experiment: tap a resonator, then add a found object and listen.  
- ✅ Save the pattern to one of the 8 memory slots.  

---

## Bottom Line  

The **KORG phase8** pushes the conversation past “analog vs. digital.” It invites you to **touch** sound in a way that most synths don’t allow, while still delivering the precision of modern electronic gear. Whether you’re a composer looking for fresh textures, a live performer craving an interactive instrument, or a sound‑designer who loves to tinker, the phase8 offers a playground that feels both **alive** and **controllable**.

---

*Explore the phase8 and its presale package directly on KORG’s site:*  

[https://www.korg.com/us/products/dj/phase8/](https://www.korg.com/us/products/dj/phase8/)
